$comment: Five ILLUSTRATIVE ERP ABAC policies for the dynamic evaluator (Issue #179).
These are EXAMPLES ONLY — AWCMS-Mini ships NO domain policies in the base.
Each object is a valid request body for POST /api/v1/access/policies. A
derived ERP application authors the ones it needs; the base stays generic.
Every attribute used here is in the server-side allow-list
parses under dsl_version 1. See ADR-0023 for the precedence model:
explicit deny wins; the RBAC permission is still required; an allow-policy
only CONSTRAINS an already-granted permission, it never creates one.

policies.$why: Ownership — a seller may edit only their OWN sales invoice. The endpoint must populate resource.ownerTenantUserId from the persisted invoice row (never a client-claimed owner). As an allow-policy it constrains the sales.invoice.update permission the subject already holds via RBAC.
policies.description: Editing a sales invoice is limited to its owner.

policies.$why: Value limit with a role carve-out — deny approving a purchase order above 50,000,000 UNLESS the subject is a finance_manager. Explicit deny overrides the RBAC approve permission for everyone else.
policies.description: Only a finance_manager may approve a purchase order over 50,000,000.

policies.$why: Posted immutability — a posted document is append-only (ADR-0005). Deny any update of a resource whose status is 'posted', tenant-wide (module/activity wildcards). This is a safety net layered over the domain's own immutability checks.
policies.description: No resource may be updated once its status is posted.

policies.$why: Environment gate — accounting exports may leave only from a trusted network. env.ipTrusted is strictly server-derived (defaults to false / fail-closed until a deployment wires a trusted-network resolver), never client-supplied.
policies.description: Ledger export is blocked from untrusted networks.

policies.$why: Lifecycle constraint — a document may be edited only while draft or pending. As an allow-policy it constrains the document.document.update permission: if the document is in any other status the allow-constraint is unsatisfied and the request is denied.
policies.description: Documents are editable only in draft or pending status.
